stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
Considering Melbourne in July, the weather is predominantly influenced by the zone of:

options.a: antarctic high pressure due to the absence of any protective land mass between south Australia and Antarctica.
options.b: subtropical high pressure, with the occasional passage of fronts originating in the adjacent zone of westerly waves.
options.c: equatorial low pressure due to the proximity of the Inter-Tropical Convergence Zone over central Australia.
options.d: disturbed temperate low pressure, bringing an almost continuous succession of fronts resulting in strong winds, low cloud and rain.
